_meta.title: PokéAPI Gen 3 & Gen 4 Override Patch
_meta.description: Manually verified corrections and additions for data categories PokéAPI gets wrong or omits entirely. Source of truth: Serebii.net. Merge this file at build time in generate-data.ts — it must survive re-runs of generate-data without being overwritten.

static_legendaries._note: PokéAPI returns empty or wrong encounter data for all of these. These must be added manually. Method is always 'static' (one-time encounter). All respawn after defeating Elite Four unless noted.

firered.name: Articuno
firered.requirement: Requires Strength and Surf to navigate. Available after obtaining HM04 Surf.

firered.name: Zapdos
firered.requirement: Requires Surf to access Route 10 power plant entrance.

firered.name: Moltres
firered.requirement: One Island accessible after defeating Blaine. Requires Strength boulder puzzle.
firered._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI often lists Moltres at Victory Road (Gen 1 location). In FRLG, Moltres was moved to Mt. Ember on One Island. This is a critical location error.

firered.name: Mewtwo
firered.requirement: Requires National Pokédex, Surf, Rock Smash, and Strength. Only accessible post-game.

firered.name: Lugia
firered.requirement: MysticTicket required (Nintendo event item). Take the right path on Navel Rock down to the island's depths.

firered.name: Ho-Oh
firered.requirement: MysticTicket required (Nintendo event item). Take the left path on Navel Rock up to the summit.

firered.name: Deoxys
firered.requirement: AuroraTicket required (Nintendo event item). Solve triangle puzzle to trigger battle. FireRed: Attack Forme.
firered._note: Deoxys reverts to Attack Forme when on a FireRed cartridge, regardless of forme when traded.

leafgreen.name: Moltres
leafgreen.requirement: Requires Strength boulder puzzle on Mt. Ember.
leafgreen._pokeapi_error: Same as FireRed — PokéAPI often shows Victory Road (Gen 1 location). Moltres is at Mt. Ember in both FRLG.

leafgreen.name: Mewtwo
leafgreen.requirement: National Pokédex required. Surf, Rock Smash, Strength needed.

leafgreen.name: Deoxys
leafgreen._note: Deoxys reverts to Defense Forme when on a LeafGreen cartridge.

ruby.name: Groudon
ruby._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI may show Groudon in Sapphire. Groudon is Ruby-exclusive, Kyogre is Sapphire-exclusive.

ruby.name: Rayquaza
ruby.requirement: Available post-game. Requires Mach Bike to navigate crumbling floors.

ruby.name: Regirock
ruby.requirement: Requires completing the Sealed Chamber puzzle first (Wailord first in party, Relicanth last, use Dig in Sealed Chamber).

ruby.name: Regice
ruby.requirement: Requires Sealed Chamber puzzle. Walk around the cave twice (clockwise against the wall).

ruby.name: Registeel
ruby.requirement: Requires Sealed Chamber puzzle. Use Flash in the center of the room.

ruby.name: Kyogre
ruby._note: Kyogre is Sapphire-exclusive. Cannot be caught in Ruby. Must trade from Sapphire or Emerald.

sapphire.name: Kyogre
sapphire._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI may show Kyogre in Ruby. Kyogre is Sapphire-exclusive, Groudon is Ruby-exclusive.

sapphire.name: Groudon
sapphire._note: Groudon is Ruby-exclusive. Cannot be caught in Sapphire. Must trade from Ruby or Emerald.

emerald.name: Kyogre
emerald.location: Marine Cave (Routes 105, 125, 127, or 129 — random weather event)
emerald.requirement: Post-Elite Four. A weather report on TV triggers either Marine Cave (heavy rain) or Terra Cave (harsh sun). Marine Cave location rotates between those four routes each time.
emerald._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI may not model the weather-cave system. Kyogre does NOT have a fixed location in Emerald.

emerald.name: Groudon
emerald.location: Terra Cave (Routes 114, 115, 116, or 118 — random weather event)
emerald.requirement: Post-Elite Four. Terra Cave (harsh sun) location rotates between those four routes.

emerald.name: Rayquaza
emerald.requirement: Story-required during delta episode. Also catchable post-game if not caught during story.

emerald.name: Regirock
emerald.requirement: Sealed Chamber puzzle required. Go left 2, down 2, use Rock Smash.

emerald.name: Regice
emerald.requirement: Sealed Chamber puzzle. Walk clockwise around the cave twice.

emerald.name: Registeel
emerald.requirement: Sealed Chamber puzzle. Use Flash in the center of the room.

emerald.name: Latias
emerald.requirement: Eon Ticket required (Nintendo event via e-Reader or Record Mixing). Holds Soul Dew. The OTHER Lati (whichever one does NOT roam) is found here.
emerald._note: In Emerald: player chooses which Lati roams (Latias or Latios) after watching TV. The OTHER one can be found on Southern Island with the Eon Ticket.

emerald.name: Latios
emerald.requirement: Eon Ticket required. The non-roaming Lati.

emerald.name: Mew
emerald.requirement: Old Sea Map required (Nintendo event item). Emerald-exclusive event Pokémon.
emerald._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI likely has no encounter data for Mew in Emerald.

diamond.name: Uxie
diamond.requirement: Available after story event at Spear Pillar.
diamond._note: Uxie does NOT respawn in Diamond/Pearl if fainted. Cannot re-battle until Platinum.

diamond.name: Azelf
diamond.requirement: Available after story event at Spear Pillar.
diamond._note: Azelf does NOT respawn in Diamond/Pearl if fainted.

diamond.name: Giratina
diamond.requirement: Post-National Dex. Navigate Turnback Cave. Diamond/Pearl: Giratina appears only here, not in Distortion World.

diamond.name: Heatran
diamond.requirement: Post-game. Buck and player explore Stark Mountain. Heatran appears after Buck takes the Magma Stone.

diamond.name: Regigigas
diamond.requirement: Must have Regirock, Regice, and Registeel in party simultaneously. These must be transferred from Gen 3 games via Pal Park in Diamond/Pearl.
diamond._note: In Diamond/Pearl, the three Regis MUST come from Gen 3 via Pal Park (or transfer). In Platinum they can be caught in-game.

diamond.name: Rotom
diamond.requirement: Must interact with the TV on the upper floor at NIGHT only (10pm–4am game time). Diamond/Pearl: only base Rotom form, no appliance forms.
diamond._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI likely missing or incorrect timing and version data for Rotom.

diamond.name: Palkia
diamond._note: Palkia is Pearl-exclusive. Trade from Pearl or obtain via event.

pearl.name: Regigigas
pearl.requirement: Regirock, Regice, Registeel in party. Must come from Gen 3 via Pal Park.

pearl.name: Rotom
pearl.requirement: Night only (10pm–4am). Interact with TV on upper floor.

pearl.name: Dialga
pearl._note: Dialga is Diamond-exclusive. Trade from Diamond.

platinum.name: Giratina
platinum.requirement: Platinum-exclusive story encounter. If not caught in Distortion World, respawns at Turnback Cave (Lv. 47) after defeating Elite Four.
platinum._note: Giratina is Origin Forme in Distortion World. Returns to Altered Forme in Turnback Cave unless holding Griseous Orb.

platinum.name: Dialga
platinum.requirement: Post-National Dex. Return to Spear Pillar after initial story events.

platinum.name: Palkia
platinum.requirement: Post-National Dex. Return to Spear Pillar.

platinum.name: Uxie
platinum._note: Unlike Diamond/Pearl, ALL legendaries in Platinum respawn after defeating Elite Four.

platinum.name: Heatran
platinum._note: Heatran is Lv. 50 in Platinum (not 70 like Diamond/Pearl).

platinum.name: Regigigas
platinum.requirement: In Platinum, Regirock/Regice/Registeel can be caught in-game (Iron Ruins, Iceberg Ruins, Steel Ruins). All three must be in party.
platinum._note: Regigigas is Lv. 1 in Platinum (not Lv. 70 like Diamond/Pearl).

platinum.name: Regirock
platinum.requirement: Platinum-exclusive in-game location. Event required in Diamond/Pearl (must import from Gen 3).
platinum._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI likely missing Platinum-specific Regi locations.

platinum.name: Rotom
platinum.requirement: Interact with TV at night OR on Monday nights. Platinum adds appliance form changes.
platinum._note: Rotom is Lv. 20 in Platinum (not 15 like Diamond/Pearl). Appliance forms unlock after National Dex from the TV in the Old Chateau.

heartgold.name: Ho-Oh
heartgold.requirement: Obtain Rainbow Wing by defeating the Radio Tower Director after clearing Team Rocket. Defeat the Kimono Girls in Ecruteak Dance Theater first. HeartGold-primary (HG players get Rainbow Wing naturally).
heartgold._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI often missing this encounter or shows wrong version.

heartgold.name: Lugia
heartgold.requirement: HeartGold: Obtain Silver Wing from the man in Pewter City (post-game). SoulSilver players get Silver Wing naturally and can encounter Lugia before Ho-Oh.

heartgold.name: Mewtwo
heartgold.requirement: Post-game, Kanto region. Requires Surf and Strength.

heartgold.name: Moltres
heartgold.requirement: Post-game, Mt. Silver region. Different from FRLG location.
heartgold._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI may show wrong location for Moltres in HGSS. It is NOT at Mt. Ember or Victory Road — it is on Mt. Silver.

heartgold.name: Kyogre
heartgold.requirement: HeartGold: Obtain Blue Orb from the man in Pewter City (post-game). Requires Surf.
heartgold._note: Groudon is SoulSilver exclusive at Embedded Tower. Kyogre is HeartGold exclusive there. Rayquaza is available in both after catching both Kyogre and Groudon.

heartgold.name: Rayquaza
heartgold.requirement: Must have caught both Kyogre (from HG) and Groudon (from SS) first. Requires trading.

heartgold.name: Suicune
heartgold.requirement: After encountering Suicune multiple times as it roams, it settles for a final battle at Route 25. HeartGold-only static encounter (in SoulSilver, Suicune roams differently and has a different encounter pattern).

heartgold.name: Gyarados (Red)
heartgold.requirement: Story-required shiny Gyarados. Always shiny. Uses standard Gyarados data but has shiny coloring.
heartgold._note: This is NOT in PokéAPI encounter data. It is a unique one-time static shiny encounter.

heartgold.name: Snorlax
heartgold.requirement: Use Pokeflute channel on Pokégear radio. Stand in front of sleeping Snorlax and use radio.

soulsilver.name: Lugia
soulsilver.requirement: SoulSilver-primary. Obtain Silver Wing from Radio Tower Director after defeating Team Rocket. Defeat Kimono Girls first.
soulsilver._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI likely missing or wrong version attribution.

soulsilver.name: Ho-Oh
soulsilver.requirement: SoulSilver: Obtain Rainbow Wing from the man in Pewter City (post-game).

soulsilver.name: Moltres
soulsilver._pokeapi_error: Moltres is on Mt. Silver in HGSS, NOT Mt. Ember or Victory Road.

soulsilver.name: Groudon
soulsilver.requirement: SoulSilver: Obtain Red Orb from man in Pewter City (post-game).

soulsilver.name: Rayquaza
soulsilver.requirement: Must have caught both Kyogre (from HG) and Groudon (from SS) first.

soulsilver.name: Snorlax
soulsilver.requirement: Use Pokéflute channel on Pokégear radio.

roaming_legendaries._note: PokéAPI cannot model roaming Pokémon properly. They have no fixed location. Flag these with method: 'roaming' and list the trigger condition. They appear in any tall grass/water encounter in the specified region.

firered._note: Only ONE of Raikou/Entei/Suicune appears per FireRed save, determined by starter choice. All three are listed here — your tracker data must include which one appears for a given save.

pokemon.name: Raikou
pokemon.trigger: Clear Team Rocket from Sevii Islands (give Ruby + Sapphire to Celio). Appears if starter was Squirtle.
pokemon.known_bug: Raikou can use Roar if trapped with Block/Mean Look, making it permanently disappear from the save file.

pokemon.name: Entei
pokemon.trigger: Clear Team Rocket from Sevii Islands. Appears if starter was Bulbasaur.
pokemon.known_bug: Entei can use Roar if trapped, making it permanently disappear. Use Master Ball.

pokemon.name: Suicune
pokemon.trigger: Clear Team Rocket from Sevii Islands. Appears if starter was Charmander.
pokemon._note: Suicune does NOT have the Roar bug. Safer to use trapping moves on.

leafgreen._note: Same one-per-save roaming mechanic as FireRed, same starter-determination logic.

pokemon.name: Raikou
pokemon.trigger: Clear Sevii Island rockets. Starter was Squirtle.

pokemon.name: Entei
pokemon.trigger: Clear Sevii Island rockets. Starter was Bulbasaur.

pokemon.name: Suicune
pokemon.trigger: Clear Sevii Island rockets. Starter was Charmander.

ruby.name: Latios
ruby.trigger: Defeat Elite Four. Watch TV in your house — choose 'blue' Pokémon. Latios roams in Ruby.
ruby._note: Ruby: Latios roams. Sapphire: Latias roams. PokéAPI often conflates these.

sapphire.name: Latias
sapphire.trigger: Defeat Elite Four. Watch TV in your house — choose 'red' Pokémon. Latias roams in Sapphire.

emerald._note: Emerald: player CHOOSES which Lati roams by answering 'red' or 'blue' after watching TV. The other can be obtained at Southern Island with Eon Ticket.

pokemon.name: Latias
pokemon.trigger: Defeat Elite Four, choose 'red' when asked on TV.

pokemon.name: Latios
pokemon.trigger: Defeat Elite Four, choose 'blue' when asked on TV.

diamond.name: Mesprit
diamond.trigger: After visiting Lake Verity during story events at Spear Pillar. First encounter triggers roaming.
diamond._note: Track via Marking Map Pokétch app. Flees immediately on turn 1.

diamond.name: Cresselia
diamond.trigger: Visit Fullmoon Island after defeating Team Galactic. Cresselia leaves a Lunar Wing and begins roaming.
diamond._note: Track via Marking Map Pokétch app.

pearl.name: Mesprit
pearl.trigger: After visiting Lake Verity during story.

platinum._note: Platinum has FIVE roaming Pokémon — by far the most of any game. PokéAPI almost certainly missing most of these.

pokemon.name: Mesprit
pokemon.trigger: After visiting Lake Verity during story.

pokemon.name: Articuno
pokemon.trigger: Obtain National Pokédex. Speak to Professor Oak after his call. All three birds are released simultaneously.
pokemon._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI missing all three bird roamers in Platinum entirely.

heartgold._note: HGSS roamers are particularly complex. Suicune does NOT fully roam in HGSS — it has a fixed final encounter after being spotted multiple times.

pokemon.name: Raikou
pokemon.trigger: After releasing the three beasts from Burned Tower (story). Roams from the start of post-Ecruteak game.

pokemon.name: Entei
pokemon.trigger: Same as Raikou — released from Burned Tower.

pokemon.name: Latias
pokemon.trigger: Defeat the Kanto Elite Four. Hear Steven's radio broadcast. HeartGold gets Latias; SoulSilver gets Latios.
pokemon._pokeapi_error: PokéAPI likely missing HGSS Lati roamers entirely.

pokemon.name: Latios
pokemon.trigger: Defeat Kanto Elite Four. Steven's radio broadcast. SoulSilver gets Latios; HeartGold gets Latias.

gift_pokemon._note: These are obtained from NPCs, not wild encounters. PokéAPI has inconsistent data for these. All are one-per-game unless noted.

firered.name: Eevee
firered._pokeapi_error: Eevee gift from Celadon Mansion is almost never in PokéAPI encounter data.

firered.name: Lapras
firered.giver: Employee after clearing Silph Co. of Rockets

firered.name: Magikarp
firered._note: One-time purchase from the Magikarp salesman.

firered.name: Tyrogue
firered.giver: Karate Master in Fighting Dojo — wait, this is the FRLG Fighting Dojo in Saffron City
firered._correction: The Fighting Dojo is in Saffron City. After defeating the Karate Master, choose Hitmonlee OR Hitmonchan (not both). Tyrogue is NOT available this way in FRLG.

firered.name: Hitmonlee
firered.giver: Karate Master. Choose Hitmonlee OR Hitmonchan.

firered.name: Hitmonchan
firered.giver: Karate Master. Choose Hitmonchan OR Hitmonlee.

firered.name: Omanyte
firered.location: Mt. Moon (Helix Fossil). Revive at Cinnabar Island Lab.

firered.name: Kabuto
firered.location: Mt. Moon (Dome Fossil). Revive at Cinnabar Island Lab.

firered.name: Aerodactyl
firered.location: Cinnabar Island (Old Amber from Mr. Fuji's aide in Pewter Museum). Revive at Cinnabar Island Lab.

firered.name: Togepi (Egg)
firered.location: One Island — man gives egg once Pokédex requirements are met.
firered._note: Egg hatches into Togepi. Hatches fairly quickly. Verify exact trigger — NPC is near the ferry or Pokémon Center.

ruby.name: Feebas (via trade only)
ruby._note: Feebas is wild on Route 119 (6 random tiles that change daily). NOT a gift. Noting here due to PokéAPI often having wrong tile data.

ruby.name: Beldum
ruby.giver: Steven Stone's note after becoming Champion
ruby._pokeapi_error: Beldum gift is almost always missing from PokéAPI.

ruby.name: Castform
ruby.giver: Weather Institute researcher after defeating Aqua hideout

ruby.name: Lileep
ruby.location: Devon Corporation (Rustboro City). Fossil found in Mirage Tower.
ruby._note: In Ruby, Root Fossil is in the top-right tile when Mirage Tower collapses. The other fossil sinks.

ruby.name: Anorith
ruby.location: Devon Corporation. Fossil found in Mirage Tower.

emerald.name: Johto Starter (one of three)
emerald.requirement: Complete the Hoenn Pokédex (own all 200 Hoenn dex entries). Prof. Birch gives one Johto starter.
emerald._pokeapi_error: Johto starters in Emerald are not in PokéAPI encounter data.
emerald._note: Choose one of: Chikorita (#152), Cyndaquil (#155), Totodile (#158).

diamond.name: Happiny (Egg)
diamond.giver: Hiker on Route 209 gives an egg that hatches into Happiny

diamond.name: Bonsly
diamond._note: NOT a gift — found via Honey Trees or Trophy Garden. Listed here as reminder that it is NOT wild on standard routes.

diamond.name: Riolu (Egg)
diamond.giver: Riley gives Riolu egg after completing Iron Island together
diamond._pokeapi_error: Riolu egg gift from Riley is not in PokéAPI encounter data.

diamond.name: Eevee
diamond._pokeapi_error: Eevee gift from Bebe is almost always missing from PokéAPI.

platinum.name: Kanto Starter (one of three)
platinum.requirement: Prof. Oak visits Pal Park after player obtains National Dex. Gives one Kanto starter.
platinum._pokeapi_error: Kanto starters in Platinum are not in PokéAPI encounter data.

heartgold.name: Togepi (Egg)
heartgold.giver: Mr. Pokémon gives Togepi Egg as part of story

heartgold.name: Lapras
heartgold.requirement: Only available on Fridays. The person's Lapras is in Union Cave B2F every Friday.
heartgold._note: This is a time-based event that PokéAPI does not model.

heartgold.name: Kanto Starter (one of three)
heartgold.requirement: Obtain all 16 gym badges (Johto + Kanto). Prof. Oak gives one Kanto starter.
heartgold._pokeapi_error: Kanto starters in HGSS almost certainly missing.

heartgold.name: Hoenn Starter (one of three)
heartgold.requirement: Defeat Red at Mt. Silver. Steven Stone gives one Hoenn starter.
heartgold._pokeapi_error: Hoenn starters in HGSS definitely missing from PokéAPI.

game_corner_pokemon._note: PokéAPI does not model Game Corner as an encounter method. These Pokémon are VERSION-EXCLUSIVE where noted and critical for living Pokédex completion.

pokemon.name: Scyther
pokemon._pokeapi_error: Scyther is FireRed Game Corner ONLY. LeafGreen has Pinsir instead. This split is critical and PokéAPI does not represent it.

pokemon.name: Pinsir
pokemon._pokeapi_error: Pinsir is LeafGreen Game Corner ONLY. FireRed has Scyther instead.

secondary_encounter_methods._note: PokéAPI largely omits these. All require National Dex unless noted.

gba_insertion_encounters._description: When specific Gen 3 GBA games are inserted in the DS's Slot-2, additional Pokémon appear in DPPt routes. PokéAPI almost certainly has no data for this.

honey_tree_encounters._description: Honey Trees in DPPt: spread honey on golden trees, wait 6-24 hours, return to encounter Pokémon. Each save file has 4 trees that can produce Munchlax.

rare.name: Munchlax
rare._note: Munchlax only appears on 4 specific honey trees per save file, determined by Trainer ID. Cannot be predicted without looking up your TID. This is essentially the only non-event source of Munchlax in Gen 4.

trophy_garden_encounters._description: Mr. Backlot's garden (Route 212, Pokémon Mansion). After National Dex, Mr. Backlot changes the daily Pokémon. Rotate daily.
trophy_garden_encounters._note: Only 2 Pokémon are available in the garden at any one time. Ask Mr. Backlot for a new Pokémon once per day.

great_marsh_encounters._description: Pastoria City Safari Zone equivalent. Some Pokémon only appear here. Pokémon available rotate by day and can be scouted via binoculars.

pokemon.name: Exeggcute
pokemon.note: Daily rotation — also requires National Dex

pokemon.name: Huntail
pokemon._note: NOT in Great Marsh; this is a placeholder correction — confirm actual list

pokemon.name: Larvitar
pokemon.note: Platinum Great Marsh only. National Dex required.

pokemon.name: Houndour
pokemon.note: Platinum Great Marsh only. National Dex required.

poke_radar_exclusives._description: Pokémon only obtainable via Poké Radar after National Dex. PokéAPI does not model this encounter method correctly.

swarm_encounters._description: Dawn/Lucas calls with daily swarm location. PokéAPI likely missing all of these. Requires National Dex.

version_exclusive_splits._note: Critical FRLG version splits that PokéAPI often conflates into single 'generation-iii' entries. These affect route encounter data directly.

pearl_only_wild.name: Tentacool
pearl_only_wild._note: Actually in both — double-check if Pearl-exclusive on specific routes

pearl_only_wild.name: Honchkrow
pearl_only_wild._note: Evolves from Murkrow (Diamond-only) — cannot be caught in Pearl

learnset_corrections._note: PokéAPI's learnset data for Gen 3 commonly conflates FRLG and RSE tutor moves. These corrections are the most critical for your learnset viewer.

frlg_exclusive_move_tutors._description: These moves are ONLY available via tutors in FireRed/LeafGreen. Not in Ruby/Sapphire. Some (marked *) are also available in Emerald but NOT in Ruby/Sapphire.
frlg_exclusive_move_tutors._impact: If your learnset viewer shows these moves as available in RSE, that is a PokéAPI error.

emerald_exclusive_move_tutors._description: Emerald has TWO sets of tutors: standard tutors (many overlap with FRLG) and Battle Frontier tutors (post-game, cost BP). Neither set is available in Ruby/Sapphire.

notable_gen3_learnset_quirks.note: False Swipe is a TM move in Gen 3 (TM54). Scyther can learn it. Important for your living Pokédex catching strategy.
Spore can ONLY be learned as a Shroomish. Once it evolves into Breloom, Spore cannot be relearned via Move Reminder in Gen 3. Plan accordingly.
Mudkip learns Hydro Pump at Lv. 42. Marshtomp and Swampert do NOT learn Hydro Pump by level-up. Transfer from Mudkip before evolving if needed.
Both could learn Thief by level-up in Gen 3 but never again. PokéAPI may or may not have this correctly.
Kabutops learns Fury Cutter as a starting move in FRLG only. Cannot learn it in RSE and not in any later games.
